name: Cholangiocarcinoma
causes: Chronic biliary inflammation is the main driver: primary sclerosing cholangitis, liver fluke infection (Opisthorchis viverrini, Clonorchis sinensis), hepatolithiasis, choledochal cysts, chronic hepatitis B or C, cirrhosis, and thorotrast exposure. Precursor lesions include biliary intraepithelial neoplasia and intraductal papillary neoplasm of the bile duct.
cause_source: WHO Classification of Tumours (Digestive System); NCI PDQ Bile Duct Cancer

name: Gallbladder cancer
causes: Strongly linked to chronic inflammation from gallstones (cholelithiasis), porcelain (calcified) gallbladder, chronic Salmonella typhi (typhoid) carriage, and gallbladder polyps; anomalous pancreaticobiliary duct junction and obesity are additional risk factors.
cause_source: IARC/WHO; NCI PDQ Gallbladder Cancer

name: Ampullary carcinoma
aliases: Cancer of the ampulla of Vater
Carcinoma of the ampulla of Vater
causes: Most cases are sporadic and arise from precursor adenomas of the ampulla of Vater; the strongest predisposing condition is familial adenomatous polyposis (germline APC), which greatly increases risk of duodenal and ampullary adenomas and carcinoma, with Lynch syndrome as a lesser hereditary contributor.
cause_source: WHO 5th ed Digestive System Tumours; NCI

name: Adrenocortical carcinoma
causes: Most cases are sporadic with no established cause; a minority arise in hereditary syndromes, notably Li-Fraumeni syndrome (germline TP53), Beckwith-Wiedemann and IGF2 overexpression (a major driver of pediatric ACC), and Lynch syndrome, with MEN1 and familial adenomatous polyposis as rarer predispositions.
cause_source: NCI PDQ / WHO Classification of Endocrine Tumours; Else et al., Endocr Rev 2014

name: Pheochromocytoma
causes: Up to 40 percent are driven by germline predisposition, most often mutations in SDHx (SDHB, SDHD, SDHC), VHL, RET (MEN2), NF1 (neurofibromatosis type 1), MAX, and TMEM127; the remainder are sporadic with no external environmental cause.
cause_source: Lenders et al., Endocrine Society Clinical Practice Guideline, J Clin Endocrinol Metab 2014; WHO Endocrine Tumours

name: Paraganglioma
aliases: glomus tumor of head and neck
causes: The most heritable of all human tumors, with germline mutations in SDHx (especially SDHD and SDHB), VHL, RET, NF1, MAX, and TMEM127; head and neck paragangliomas are additionally promoted by chronic hypoxia such as high-altitude residence.
cause_source: Fishbein & Nathanson, Cancer Genet 2012; Cerecer-Gil et al., Clin Cancer Res 2010 (high-altitude/SDHB)

name: Medullary thyroid carcinoma
causes: About a quarter are hereditary, caused by germline activating mutations in the RET proto-oncogene in MEN2A, MEN2B, and familial MTC; the remaining sporadic cases carry somatic RET or RAS mutations and have no established environmental cause.
cause_source: Wells et al., ATA Guidelines, Thyroid 2015; WHO Classification of Endocrine Tumours

name: Anaplastic thyroid carcinoma
causes: Frequently arises by dedifferentiation from a preexisting well-differentiated or poorly differentiated papillary or follicular thyroid carcinoma; long-standing goiter and prior radiation exposure are contributing risk factors, and TP53 plus BRAF/RAS mutations accumulate during progression.
cause_source: WHO Classification of Endocrine Tumours 2022; Molinaro et al., Nat Rev Endocrinol 2017

name: Hurthle cell thyroid carcinoma
causes: Ionizing radiation exposure to the head and neck, especially in childhood, is an established risk factor; chronic autoimmune thyroiditis (Hashimoto) and iodine deficiency are associated, and rare familial nonmedullary thyroid cancer clustering occurs, but most cases are sporadic.
cause_source: WebSearch (Mayo Clinic, StatPearls NBK568736, Cleveland Clinic 2024)

name: Uveal melanoma
causes: Largely of unknown cause and, unlike cutaneous melanoma, not clearly linked to UV exposure; established risk factors are light iris/skin color, oculodermal melanocytosis (nevus of Ota), and BAP1 tumor predisposition syndrome (germline BAP1), with driver mutations in GNAQ/GNA11.
cause_source: Kaliki & Shields, Eye 2017; Rai et al./BAP1 - Harbour et al., Science 2010

name: Merkel cell carcinoma
causes: Two established causal pathways: clonal integration of Merkel cell polyomavirus (about 80 percent of cases) and UV-induced DNA damage in virus-negative tumors; chronic immunosuppression (HIV, transplant, older age) is a strong additional risk factor.
cause_source: Feng et al., Science 2008 (MCPyV); Harms et al., Nat Rev Clin Oncol 2018

name: Sebaceous carcinoma
causes: Most periocular cases are sporadic, but a significant subset arises in Muir-Torre syndrome, a variant of Lynch syndrome caused by germline mismatch repair mutations (MSH2, MLH1); UV exposure and prior radiation are additional risk factors.
cause_source: Kyllo et al., Dermatol Surg 2015; John & Schwartz, Muir-Torre syndrome reviews

name: Dermatofibrosarcoma protuberans
causes: Defined by a somatic COL1A1-PDGFB gene fusion but has no established environmental or hereditary cause; the vast majority are sporadic, with prior trauma or scar reported only anecdotally and inconsistently.
cause_source: Llombart et al., Semin Diagn Pathol 2013; WHO Classification of Skin Tumours 2018

name: Cutaneous squamous cell carcinoma
causes: Chronic cumulative ultraviolet (sun) exposure is the dominant cause; other factors include immunosuppression (organ transplant, HIV), human papillomavirus, chronic wounds and scars (Marjolin ulcer), arsenic and radiation exposure, and genodermatoses such as xeroderma pigmentosum and epidermolysis bullosa.
cause_source: WHO Classification of Skin Tumours; NCI PDQ Skin Cancer

name: Mucosal melanoma
causes: Cause is largely unknown; unlike cutaneous melanoma it is NOT associated with UV exposure and lacks a UV mutational signature, arising sporadically from mucosal melanocytes of the head/neck, anogenital, and other sites.
cause_source: Nassar & Tan, Semin Cancer Biol 2020; Carvajal et al., Cancer 2012

name: Acral lentiginous melanoma
causes: Cause is largely unknown; it occurs on palms, soles, and nail units and is NOT strongly linked to UV exposure, showing low UV mutational burden, with mechanical trauma proposed but not established as a cause.
cause_source: Bastian et al./Newell et al., Nat Commun 2020; WHO Classification of Skin Tumours

name: Thymoma
causes: Cause not established; most thymomas are sporadic with no known environmental or inherited cause. They are strongly associated with autoimmune paraneoplastic conditions, most notably myasthenia gravis, but that association is not itself an established cause.
cause_source: WHO 5th ed

name: Thymic carcinoma
causes: Cause not established; thymic carcinoma is sporadic with no proven environmental, infectious, or inherited etiology, although a minority of EBV-associated lymphoepithelial subtypes occur.
cause_source: WHO 5th ed

name: Malignant mesothelioma
causes: Asbestos exposure is the established cause in the great majority of cases, with a long latency of decades; erionite fiber exposure and prior chest radiation are additional causes, and germline BAP1 mutation predisposes.
cause_source: IARC Monographs (asbestos); WHO Classification of Thoracic Tumours

name: Peritoneal mesothelioma
causes: Asbestos exposure is the principal established cause; erionite exposure and prior therapeutic radiation are additional recognized risk factors, and germline BAP1 mutation predisposes to mesothelioma.
cause_source: NCI / WHO 5th ed

name: Pseudomyxoma peritonei
causes: Nearly always caused by rupture of a low-grade appendiceal mucinous neoplasm (less often a mucinous tumor of ovary, colon, or other sites) that seeds the peritoneum with mucin-producing epithelium; the underlying appendiceal neoplasm itself is sporadic with no established external cause.
cause_source: WHO 5th ed Digestive System Tumours; PMID 26260910

name: Appendiceal cancer
causes: Cause not established; most appendiceal tumors, including mucinous neoplasms and appendiceal neuroendocrine tumors, arise sporadically with no confirmed environmental or hereditary etiology.
cause_source: NCI PDQ; WHO Classification of Digestive System Tumours

name: Small bowel adenocarcinoma
causes: Predisposed by inherited syndromes (Lynch syndrome, familial adenomatous polyposis, Peutz-Jeghers syndrome) and by chronic small bowel inflammation, notably Crohn disease and celiac disease.
cause_source: WHO Classification of Digestive System Tumours; NCI PDQ Small Intestine Cancer

name: Anal cancer
aliases: Squamous cell carcinoma of the anus
causes: Caused predominantly by persistent high-risk human papillomavirus (especially HPV-16) infection; risk is amplified by HIV infection, immunosuppression, receptive anal intercourse, and smoking. Precursor lesion is anal intraepithelial neoplasia.
cause_source: IARC Monographs (HPV); NCI PDQ Anal Cancer

name: Vaginal cancer
causes: Most squamous vaginal cancers are caused by persistent high-risk human papillomavirus infection; clear cell adenocarcinoma of the vagina is linked to in utero diethylstilbestrol (DES) exposure. Precursor is vaginal intraepithelial neoplasia.
cause_source: IARC Monographs (HPV); NCI PDQ Vaginal Cancer

name: Vulvar cancer
aliases: squamous cell carcinoma of the vulva
causes: Two pathways: high-risk HPV infection (with usual-type vulvar intraepithelial neoplasia) and HPV-independent disease arising in chronic inflammatory dermatoses such as lichen sclerosus with differentiated vulvar intraepithelial neoplasia; smoking and immunosuppression add risk.
cause_source: WHO Classification of Female Genital Tumours; NCI PDQ Vulvar Cancer

name: Penile cancer
causes: Caused by high-risk HPV infection in a large fraction of cases; other risk factors include phimosis with chronic inflammation, lack of neonatal circumcision, lichen sclerosus, smoking, and poor hygiene. Precursor is penile intraepithelial neoplasia.
cause_source: IARC Monographs (HPV); NCI PDQ Penile Cancer

name: Urethral cancer
causes: Associated with high-risk HPV infection (especially distal urethral squamous cancers), chronic urethral inflammation, urethral strictures, and recurrent urinary tract infections; a history of bladder cancer also raises risk.
cause_source: NCI PDQ Urethral Cancer; WHO Classification of Urinary Tumours

name: Male breast cancer
causes: Predisposed by germline BRCA2 (and BRCA1) mutations, Klinefelter syndrome, conditions of estrogen excess or androgen deficiency (obesity, cirrhosis, testicular disorders), prior chest radiation, and family history.
cause_source: NCI PDQ Male Breast Cancer; ASCO

name: Inflammatory breast cancer
causes: Cause not established; it is an aggressive clinical presentation of breast carcinoma without a specific etiologic factor, though obesity and general breast cancer risk factors apply and it is usually sporadic.
cause_source: NCI PDQ Breast Cancer; ACS

name: Salivary gland carcinoma
causes: Prior ionizing radiation to the head and neck is the best-established risk factor; some subtypes are associated with defined genetic fusions rather than external causes, and most cases are otherwise sporadic.
cause_source: WHO Classification of Head and Neck Tumours; NCI PDQ Salivary Gland Cancer

name: Adenoid cystic carcinoma
causes: Cause not established; salivary and other adenoid cystic carcinomas are largely sporadic, with prior ionizing radiation to the head and neck the only recognized minor risk factor.
cause_source: WHO 5th ed

name: Mucoepidermoid carcinoma
causes: Mostly sporadic; prior ionizing radiation exposure to the head and neck (for example therapeutic radiotherapy or atomic bomb survivors) is the main established risk factor for this salivary gland carcinoma.
cause_source: PMID 9665263 / WHO 5th ed

name: Acinic cell carcinoma
causes: Largely sporadic with no consistently established cause; prior ionizing radiation to the head and neck is a recognized minor risk factor for salivary gland carcinomas.
cause_source: WHO 5th ed

name: Nasopharyngeal carcinoma
aliases: keratinizing squamous cell carcinoma of nasopharynx
basaloid squamous cell carcinoma of nasopharynx
causes: Epstein-Barr virus infection is the central causal agent, acting together with dietary salt-preserved fish, tobacco smoking, inherited HLA class I susceptibility, and family history, which together produce its distinctive geographic distribution.
cause_source: IARC / PMC5597737

name: Sinonasal undifferentiated carcinoma
causes: Cause largely not established; most cases are sporadic, though occupational exposures such as wood dust and nickel are implicated in sinonasal cancers broadly and a subset is IDH2-mutant.
cause_source: WHO 5th ed

name: Esthesioneuroblastoma
causes: No established cause; almost all cases are sporadic and no consistent risk factor is proven. Occupational exposure to wood dust, solvents, and heavy metals has been proposed but not confirmed, and there is no inherited predisposition.
cause_source: NCI PDQ / StatPearls (esthesioneuroblastoma)

name: NUT carcinoma
causes: Cause not established; NUT carcinoma arises from a sporadic somatic NUTM1 rearrangement (usually BRD4::NUTM1) with no known environmental, lifestyle, or inherited risk factor.
cause_source: PMC8966081 / WHO 5th ed

name: Small cell carcinoma of the ovary hypercalcemic type
aliases: malignant rhabdoid tumor of the ovary
causes: Driven by germline or somatic inactivating mutations of SMARCA4 (BRG1), and germline carriers define a familial predisposition (rhabdoid tumor predisposition syndrome type 2); the underlying etiology beyond this SWI/SNF defect is otherwise unknown, with no established environmental, infectious, or hormonal cause.
cause_source: WHO Classification of Female Genital Tumours 5th ed; Witkowski et al. Nat Genet 2014

name: Granulosa cell tumor of the ovary
causes: Cause not established; adult type is characterized by a somatic FOXL2 mutation but has no known inherited or environmental cause, and juvenile type can occur in enchondromatosis syndromes (Ollier disease, Maffucci syndrome). Generally sporadic.
cause_source: WHO Classification of Female Genital Tumours

name: Ovarian germ cell tumor
causes: Cause largely not established; these tumors arise from primordial germ cells and are usually sporadic, with gonadal dysgenesis (dysgenetic gonads, especially with Y chromosome material) being a recognized predisposing condition for some subtypes.
cause_source: WHO Classification of Female Genital Tumours; NCI PDQ

name: Ovarian clear cell carcinoma
aliases: clear cell adenocarcinoma of the ovary
causes: Strongly linked to endometriosis and endometriotic cysts as a precursor lesion, with additional risk from endometriosis-associated inflammation; a minority arise in Lynch syndrome (mismatch repair) carriers.
cause_source: WHO Classification of Female Genital Tumours 5th ed; Wiegand et al. NEJM 2010

name: Low grade serous ovarian carcinoma
causes: Cause not established; arises from serous borderline (atypical proliferative) tumor as a precursor lesion but has no confirmed inherited or environmental etiologic cause, and is generally sporadic.
cause_source: WHO Classification of Female Genital Tumours

name: Uterine leiomyosarcoma
causes: Largely sporadic with no established cause; recognized risk factors include prior pelvic radiation and hereditary leiomyomatosis and renal cell cancer syndrome (germline fumarate hydratase), and tamoxifen exposure is a weaker association. Most cases arise de novo, not from benign leiomyomas.
cause_source: WHO Classification of Female Genital Tumours 5th ed; NCI PDQ Uterine Sarcoma

name: Uterine carcinosarcoma
causes: Risk factors mirror endometrial carcinoma (obesity, unopposed estrogen exposure, nulliparity) and prior pelvic radiation and tamoxifen use are established predisposing exposures; it is regarded as a metaplastic (dedifferentiated) high-grade endometrial carcinoma.
cause_source: WHO Classification of Female Genital Tumours 5th ed; NCI PDQ

name: Gestational trophoblastic disease
causes: Arises from abnormal fertilization producing hydatidiform mole (complete moles from androgenetic diploidy, partial moles from triploid dispermic fertilization); a prior molar pregnancy and extremes of maternal age are risk factors, and rare familial recurrent hydatidiform mole is caused by NLRP7 or KHDC3L mutations.
cause_source: WHO Classification of Female Genital Tumours; NCI PDQ Gestational Trophoblastic Disease

name: Endometrial stromal sarcoma
causes: Cause not established; defined by recurrent gene fusions (for example JAZF1::SUZ12) but has no confirmed inherited or environmental cause, and tamoxifen use and unopposed estrogen have been weakly implicated. Generally sporadic.
cause_source: WHO Classification of Female Genital Tumours

name: Testicular germ cell tumor
causes: Predisposed by cryptorchidism (undescended testis), personal or family history of testicular cancer, testicular dysgenesis, infertility, and Klinefelter syndrome; germ cell neoplasia in situ is the precursor lesion.
cause_source: NCI PDQ Testicular Cancer; WHO Classification of Urinary and Male Genital Tumours

name: Seminoma
causes: Same predisposing factors as other testicular germ cell tumors, chiefly cryptorchidism, prior testicular tumor, family history, and testicular dysgenesis; arises from germ cell neoplasia in situ.
cause_source: NCI PDQ Testicular Cancer; WHO Classification

name: Nonseminomatous germ cell tumor
causes: Shares testicular germ cell tumor risk factors, notably cryptorchidism, family or personal history, and testicular dysgenesis; develops from germ cell neoplasia in situ.
cause_source: NCI PDQ Testicular Cancer; WHO Classification

name: Extragonadal germ cell tumor
causes: Predisposing conditions include Klinefelter syndrome (associated with mediastinal germ cell tumors) and gonadal dysgenesis; these arise from misplaced primordial germ cells along the midline and are otherwise largely of unestablished cause.
cause_source: NCI PDQ Extragonadal Germ Cell Tumors

name: Renal medullary carcinoma
causes: Occurs almost exclusively in individuals with sickle cell trait or other sickle hemoglobinopathies (rarely sickle cell disease or hemoglobin SC), the defining predisposing condition.
cause_source: WHO Classification of Urinary and Male Genital Tumours 5th ed; Davis et al. Am J Surg Pathol 1995

name: Chromophobe renal cell carcinoma
causes: Mostly sporadic with no established environmental cause; a fraction arises in Birt-Hogg-Dube syndrome (germline FLCN mutation), which predisposes to chromophobe and hybrid oncocytic tumors.
cause_source: WHO Classification of Urinary and Male Genital Tumours 5th ed; Pavlovich et al. Am J Surg Pathol 2002

name: Papillary renal cell carcinoma
causes: Hereditary forms have defined causes: hereditary papillary RCC from germline MET mutations (type 1) and hereditary leiomyomatosis and RCC from germline fumarate hydratase mutations (type 2); acquired cystic kidney disease and end-stage renal disease are acquired risk factors, but most cases are sporadic.
cause_source: NCI PDQ Kidney Cancer; WHO Classification of Urinary Tumours

name: Collecting duct carcinoma of the kidney
causes: Cause not established; this aggressive renal cancer arises from the distal collecting duct with no confirmed inherited or environmental etiology, and is generally sporadic.
cause_source: WHO Classification of Urinary Tumours; NCI PDQ

name: Translocation renal cell carcinoma
causes: Prior exposure to cytotoxic chemotherapy in childhood is the only established risk factor; the tumors are driven by MiT family (TFE3/TFEB) gene fusions, but most arise sporadically without a known external cause.
cause_source: WebSearch (Translational Cancer Research 2024, PMC11651741; Front Oncol 2021)

name: Wilms tumor
causes: Mostly sporadic, but a subset is driven by germline predisposition including WT1-related syndromes (WAGR, Denys-Drash), Beckwith-Wiedemann syndrome (11p15 imprinting), and other overgrowth and DICER1 or Perlman syndromes; nephrogenic rests are the precursor lesion.
cause_source: WHO Classification of Paediatric Tumours; NCI PDQ Wilms Tumor

name: Urachal carcinoma
causes: Arises from persistent embryonic urachal remnants at the bladder dome; no definitive environmental or hereditary risk factors are established, and it is otherwise considered sporadic.
cause_source: WebSearch (NORD Urachal Cancer; Front Oncol 2023)

name: Sarcomatoid carcinoma
causes: Cause not established as a distinct entity; it represents dedifferentiation of an underlying epithelial carcinoma (for example of lung, kidney, or urothelium), so its risk factors are those of the parent carcinoma rather than a specific separate cause.
cause_source: WHO Classification of Tumours (organ-specific volumes)

name: Large cell neuroendocrine carcinoma
causes: When pulmonary, it is strongly caused by tobacco smoking, similar to small cell lung cancer; large cell neuroendocrine carcinomas at other sites are largely of unestablished cause.
cause_source: WHO Classification of Thoracic Tumours; IARC Monographs (tobacco)

name: Small cell lung cancer
causes: Overwhelmingly caused by tobacco smoking; radon exposure and occupational carcinogens are additional causes. It very rarely occurs in never-smokers.
cause_source: IARC Monographs (tobacco, radon); NCI PDQ Small Cell Lung Cancer

name: Pulmonary carcinoid tumor
causes: Cause largely not established; most pulmonary carcinoids are not linked to smoking, and a minority occur in the setting of multiple endocrine neoplasia type 1 (germline MEN1 mutation) or arise from diffuse idiopathic pulmonary neuroendocrine cell hyperplasia as a precursor.
cause_source: WHO Classification of Thoracic Tumours; NCI PDQ

name: Pleuropulmonary blastoma
causes: Frequently caused by an inherited DICER1 germline mutation (DICER1 tumor predisposition syndrome, autosomal dominant), with a second somatic DICER1 hit; a subset is sporadic.
cause_source: PMC4209484 / Orphanet

name: Hepatoblastoma
causes: The most common pediatric liver cancer, strongly associated with hereditary syndromes including Beckwith-Wiedemann syndrome and familial adenomatous polyposis (germline APC), and with prematurity and very low birth weight; most sporadic cases have activating CTNNB1 (beta-catenin) mutations.
cause_source: WHO 5th ed; NCI PDQ Childhood Liver Cancer

name: Fibrolamellar hepatocellular carcinoma
causes: Occurs in adolescents and young adults without cirrhosis or underlying liver disease; it is defined by a somatic DNAJB1-PRKACA fusion but has no established etiologic risk factor, virus, or predisposing exposure, so the cause is genuinely unknown.
cause_source: WHO 5th ed; PMID 24578576

name: Pancreatic neuroendocrine tumor
causes: Most are sporadic with no established environmental cause, but a substantial minority arise in hereditary syndromes: multiple endocrine neoplasia type 1 (MEN1), von Hippel-Lindau disease, neurofibromatosis type 1, and tuberous sclerosis all predispose to pancreatic neuroendocrine tumors.
cause_source: WHO 5th ed; NCI

name: Gastrointestinal neuroendocrine tumor
causes: Most are sporadic with no established cause, but recognized predisposing conditions include multiple endocrine neoplasia type 1 (MEN1), and for gastric types, chronic atrophic gastritis with hypergastrinemia (autoimmune or Zollinger-Ellison syndrome).
cause_source: WHO Classification of Digestive System Tumours; NCI PDQ

name: Acinar cell carcinoma of the pancreas
causes: Predominantly sporadic with no established cause; weak associations include tobacco smoking and rare occurrence within familial cancer syndromes (familial adenomatous polyposis, Lynch syndrome, Peutz-Jeghers), but for most cases the etiology is not established.
cause_source: WHO 5th ed; PMID 22475742

name: Pancreatoblastoma
causes: Rare embryonal pancreatic tumor of childhood associated with Beckwith-Wiedemann syndrome and with familial adenomatous polyposis (APC/Wnt pathway alterations); the majority of sporadic cases have no established external cause.
cause_source: WHO 5th ed; PMID 22475742

name: Gastrointestinal stromal tumor
causes: Most are sporadic and driven by somatic KIT or PDGFRA mutations with no external cause, but predisposing conditions exist: neurofibromatosis type 1, Carney triad, Carney-Stratakis syndrome, and rare familial germline KIT/PDGFRA mutations all increase risk.
cause_source: WHO 5th ed; NCI

name: Osteosarcoma
causes: Predisposed by hereditary cancer syndromes (hereditary retinoblastoma with germline RB1, Li-Fraumeni syndrome with germline TP53, Rothmund-Thomson, Werner, Bloom syndromes), prior therapeutic radiation, and Paget disease of bone in older adults.
cause_source: NCI PDQ Osteosarcoma; WHO Classification of Bone and Soft Tissue Tumours

name: Ewing sarcoma
causes: Sporadic with no established environmental or hereditary cause; defined by a somatic EWSR1::FLI1 (or related ETS) fusion that is an oncogenic driver rather than an inherited risk factor, and incidence is notably higher in people of European ancestry than African or Asian ancestry.
cause_source: WHO Classification of Tumours: Soft Tissue and Bone (5th ed); Grunewald et al., Nat Rev Dis Primers 2018

name: Chondrosarcoma
causes: Most conventional cases are sporadic, but a subset arises from precursor cartilage lesions such as enchondromas (Ollier disease, Maffucci syndrome) and osteochondromas (hereditary multiple exostoses due to EXT1/EXT2 mutations).
cause_source: WHO Classification of Bone and Soft Tissue Tumours; NCI PDQ

name: Chordoma
causes: Arises from notochordal remnants along the axial skeleton; a benign notochordal cell tumor is a precursor, and germline duplication of the T (brachyury/TBXT) gene predisposes in familial chordoma. Most cases are otherwise sporadic.
cause_source: WHO Classification of Bone and Soft Tissue Tumours; NCI

name: Rhabdomyosarcoma
causes: Mostly sporadic, but predisposed by hereditary syndromes including Li-Fraumeni syndrome (germline TP53), neurofibromatosis type 1, DICER1 syndrome, Costello syndrome, and Beckwith-Wiedemann syndrome.
cause_source: NCI PDQ Childhood Rhabdomyosarcoma; WHO Classification

name: Alveolar rhabdomyosarcoma
causes: Cause not established; arises sporadically. The defining PAX3::FOXO1 or PAX7::FOXO1 fusion is a somatic event, not an inherited or environmental cause; a minority of rhabdomyosarcomas occur in cancer predisposition syndromes such as Li-Fraumeni (germline TP53), DICER1, and neurofibromatosis type 1.
cause_source: WHO Classification of Soft Tissue and Bone Tumours (5th ed, 2020); GeneReviews Li-Fraumeni Syndrome (NCBI Bookshelf NBK1311)

name: Embryonal rhabdomyosarcoma
causes: Mostly sporadic with no established cause, but this is the rhabdomyosarcoma subtype most strongly linked to germline cancer predisposition syndromes, including Li-Fraumeni (germline TP53), DICER1 syndrome, Costello syndrome (HRAS), neurofibromatosis type 1, and Beckwith-Wiedemann syndrome.
cause_source: WHO Classification of Soft Tissue and Bone Tumours (5th ed, 2020); GeneReviews DICER1 Tumor Predisposition (NCBI Bookshelf)

name: Synovial sarcoma
causes: Cause not established; arises sporadically. The defining SS18::SSX gene fusion is a somatic event, not an inherited or environmental cause.
cause_source: WHO Classification of Soft Tissue and Bone Tumours (5th ed, 2020)

name: Liposarcoma
causes: Cause not established; liposarcomas are generally sporadic soft tissue tumors defined by characteristic genetic changes (for example MDM2 amplification) without a confirmed inherited or environmental etiology.
cause_source: WHO Classification of Bone and Soft Tissue Tumours

name: Myxoid liposarcoma
causes: Cause not established; arises sporadically. The defining FUS::DDIT3 (or EWSR1::DDIT3) fusion is a somatic event, not an inherited or environmental cause.
cause_source: WHO Classification of Soft Tissue and Bone Tumours (5th ed, 2020)

name: Leiomyosarcoma
causes: Most cases are sporadic with no established cause, but a subset arises in hereditary cancer syndromes, chiefly Li-Fraumeni syndrome (germline TP53) and hereditary retinoblastoma (germline RB1); prior therapeutic radiation is also a recognized risk factor for radiation-associated leiomyosarcoma.
cause_source: Ballinger et al., Clin Cancer Res 2024 (PMID 39150540); WHO Classification of Soft Tissue and Bone Tumours (5th ed, 2020)

name: Undifferentiated pleomorphic sarcoma
causes: Most cases are sporadic with no established cause, but a recognized subset is radiation-associated, arising years after therapeutic radiation to a prior site; occurrence in Li-Fraumeni syndrome (germline TP53) is also reported.
cause_source: WHO Classification of Soft Tissue and Bone Tumours (5th ed, 2020); NCI PDQ Adult Soft Tissue Sarcoma

name: Fibrosarcoma
causes: Cause largely not established; adult fibrosarcoma is usually sporadic, and infantile fibrosarcoma is defined by an ETV6::NTRK3 fusion. Prior radiation is a recognized cause of some secondary fibrosarcomas.
cause_source: WHO Classification of Bone and Soft Tissue Tumours

name: Angiosarcoma
causes: Several established etiologic factors exist: prior therapeutic radiation (including secondary breast angiosarcoma), chronic lymphedema (Stewart-Treves syndrome after mastectomy), and industrial vinyl chloride or Thorotrast exposure for hepatic angiosarcoma; ultraviolet exposure is linked to cutaneous head and neck angiosarcoma.
cause_source: WHO Classification of Soft Tissue and Bone Tumours (5th ed, 2020); Young et al., angiosarcoma review PMC6895451

name: Epithelioid hemangioendothelioma
causes: Cause not established; arises sporadically. The defining WWTR1::CAMTA1 (or YAP1::TFE3) fusion is a somatic event, not an inherited or environmental cause.
cause_source: WHO Classification of Soft Tissue and Bone Tumours (5th ed, 2020)

name: Kaposi sarcoma
causes: Caused by human herpesvirus 8 (HHV-8 / KSHV) infection, which is necessary for all clinical forms; disease develops in the setting of immunosuppression, most notably HIV coinfection (amplifying risk several hundred to thousand fold) and iatrogenic immunosuppression after solid organ transplant.
cause_source: IARC Monographs Volume 100B (HHV-8); WHO Classification of Soft Tissue and Bone Tumours (5th ed, 2020)

name: Alveolar soft part sarcoma
causes: Cause not established; arises sporadically. The defining ASPSCR1::TFE3 fusion is a somatic event, not an inherited or environmental cause.
cause_source: WHO Classification of Soft Tissue and Bone Tumours (5th ed, 2020)

name: Clear cell sarcoma
causes: Cause not established; this melanocytic soft tissue sarcoma is defined by an EWSR1::ATF1 fusion but has no known inherited or environmental cause, and is sporadic.
cause_source: WHO Classification of Bone and Soft Tissue Tumours

name: Desmoplastic small round cell tumor
causes: Cause not established; arises sporadically. The defining EWSR1::WT1 fusion is a somatic event, not an inherited or environmental cause.
cause_source: WHO Classification of Soft Tissue and Bone Tumours (5th ed, 2020)

name: Epithelioid sarcoma
causes: Cause not established; arises sporadically. The characteristic biallelic somatic inactivation of SMARCB1 (INI1) is an acquired event, not an inherited or environmental cause.
cause_source: WHO Classification of Soft Tissue and Bone Tumours (5th ed, 2020)

name: Solitary fibrous tumor
causes: Cause not established; defined by a NAB2::STAT6 fusion but without a confirmed inherited or environmental etiologic factor. Generally sporadic.
cause_source: WHO Classification of Bone and Soft Tissue Tumours

name: Malignant peripheral nerve sheath tumor
causes: About half arise in patients with neurofibromatosis type 1 (germline NF1), typically via malignant transformation of a preexisting plexiform neurofibroma; prior therapeutic radiation is another established cause, and the remainder are sporadic.
cause_source: Evans et al., J Med Genet 2002; WHO Classification of Soft Tissue Tumours

name: Desmoid tumor
causes: Predisposed by germline APC mutations in familial adenomatous polyposis (Gardner syndrome); sporadic desmoids are driven by CTNNB1 (beta-catenin) mutations and are associated with prior trauma or surgery, pregnancy, and estrogen exposure.
cause_source: WHO Classification of Bone and Soft Tissue Tumours; NCCN

name: Inflammatory myofibroblastic tumor
causes: Cause not established; arises sporadically. It is driven by somatic kinase gene rearrangements (most often ALK, also ROS1, NTRK, PDGFRB), which are acquired events, not an inherited or environmental cause.
cause_source: WHO Classification of Soft Tissue and Bone Tumours (5th ed, 2020)

name: Extraskeletal myxoid chondrosarcoma
causes: Cause not established; arises sporadically. The defining EWSR1::NR4A3 (or TAF15::NR4A3) fusion is a somatic event, not an inherited or environmental cause.
cause_source: WHO Classification of Soft Tissue and Bone Tumours (5th ed, 2020)

name: Myxofibrosarcoma
causes: Most cases are sporadic with no established cause and complex genomic changes; a recognized subset is radiation-associated, arising years after therapeutic radiation to the region.
cause_source: WHO Classification of Soft Tissue and Bone Tumours (5th ed, 2020); NCI PDQ Adult Soft Tissue Sarcoma

name: Pleomorphic rhabdomyosarcoma
causes: Cause not established; arises sporadically in adults with complex genomic changes and no defining fusion, and no consistent inherited or environmental cause.
cause_source: WHO Classification of Soft Tissue and Bone Tumours (5th ed, 2020)

name: Spindle cell sarcoma
causes: Cause not established; spindle cell sarcoma is a morphologic category encompassing several entities with no single confirmed etiology, and most are sporadic (some secondary to prior radiation).
cause_source: WHO Classification of Bone and Soft Tissue Tumours

name: PEComa
causes: A subset arises in the tuberous sclerosis complex (germline TSC1/TSC2 mutations); most sporadic PEComas have no established inherited or environmental cause.
cause_source: WHO Classification of Bone and Soft Tissue Tumours

name: Glioblastoma
causes: Cause not established for the vast majority; the only firmly established environmental risk factor is prior ionizing radiation to the head, and rare cases occur in hereditary syndromes (Li-Fraumeni, Lynch/constitutional mismatch repair deficiency, neurofibromatosis). Otherwise sporadic.
cause_source: WHO Classification of CNS Tumours; NCI PDQ

name: Diffuse intrinsic pontine glioma
causes: Cause not established; this pediatric brainstem glioma is defined by histone H3 K27 mutations and has no known inherited or environmental cause. Sporadic.
cause_source: WHO Classification of CNS Tumours; NCI

name: Ependymoma
causes: Mostly sporadic; the main recognized hereditary predisposition is neurofibromatosis type 2 (germline NF2), which is associated with spinal ependymomas. No established environmental cause.
cause_source: WHO Classification of CNS Tumours; NCI PDQ

name: Medulloblastoma
causes: Mostly sporadic, but predisposed by hereditary syndromes: Gorlin (nevoid basal cell carcinoma) syndrome and other SHH-pathway germline defects for SHH-activated tumors, and germline APC (Turcot syndrome) for WNT-activated tumors, as well as Li-Fraumeni syndrome.
cause_source: WHO Classification of CNS Tumours; NCI PDQ

name: Oligodendroglioma
causes: Cause not established; defined by IDH mutation and 1p/19q codeletion but with no confirmed inherited or environmental cause. Prior brain irradiation is a general risk factor for gliomas; otherwise sporadic.
cause_source: WHO Classification of CNS Tumours

name: Pilocytic astrocytoma
causes: Most are sporadic with no known cause, but neurofibromatosis type 1 (germline NF1 mutation) strongly predisposes to pilocytic astrocytoma, particularly optic pathway glioma, in which roughly 15 to 20 percent of children with NF1 develop an optic pathway tumor.
cause_source: WHO CNS5; NF1 optic pathway glioma literature (IOVS/Neuro-Oncology reviews)

name: Atypical teratoid rhabdoid tumor
causes: Germline SMARCB1 (or rarely SMARCA4) loss-of-function variants in rhabdoid tumor predisposition syndrome underlie a substantial minority of cases; the remainder are sporadic from biallelic somatic SMARCB1 inactivation, with no established environmental cause.
cause_source: WHO CNS5 2021; Eaton et al., Pediatr Blood Cancer 2011

name: Craniopharyngioma
causes: Cause not established; adamantinomatous type arises from embryonic Rathke pouch epithelial remnants with CTNNB1 mutations, and papillary type carries BRAF V600E mutations, but neither has a known inherited or environmental cause. Sporadic.
cause_source: WHO Classification of CNS Tumours

name: Pineoblastoma
aliases: Pineal parenchymal tumor of poor differentiation
causes: Predisposition includes germline RB1 mutation (trilateral retinoblastoma) and germline DICER1 pathogenic variants (DICER1 syndrome), with additional familial cases linked to RB1 and DICER1 pathway loss; most cases are otherwise sporadic.
cause_source: de Kock et al., Acta Neuropathol 2014; WHO CNS5 2021

name: Meningioma
causes: Prior ionizing radiation to the head is an established cause; neurofibromatosis type 2 (germline NF2) predisposes to multiple meningiomas, and hormonal factors are associated. Many cases are otherwise sporadic.
cause_source: WHO Classification of CNS Tumours; NCI

name: Hemangioblastoma
causes: About one quarter arise in von Hippel-Lindau disease from a germline VHL tumor suppressor mutation, which is the only established genetic cause; the remaining sporadic tumors have no established cause.
cause_source: WHO CNS5; VHL disease reviews (StatPearls, Springer Child's Nerv Syst)

name: Neuroblastoma
causes: Mostly sporadic, but familial neuroblastoma is driven by germline ALK activating mutations or PHOX2B mutations, and it also arises in predisposition syndromes (e.g., congenital central hypoventilation and Hirschsprung disease with PHOX2B); no strong environmental cause is established.
cause_source: Mosse et al., Nature 2008; Maris, N Engl J Med 2010

name: Retinoblastoma
causes: Caused by biallelic RB1 inactivation; heritable (bilateral or familial) disease results from a germline RB1 loss-of-function mutation, whereas unilateral sporadic disease arises from two somatic RB1 hits.
cause_source: Knudson, PNAS 1971; WHO Classification of Tumours

name: Choroid plexus carcinoma
causes: Strongly linked to Li-Fraumeni syndrome from a germline TP53 mutation, especially in young children; germline TP53 alteration is found in a large proportion of pediatric cases and any child with this tumor warrants TP53 testing.
cause_source: Frontiers Oncol 2021; Tabori et al., germline TP53 choroid plexus carcinoma studies

name: Gliosarcoma
causes: A variant of IDH-wildtype glioblastoma that is essentially always sporadic with no established cause; prior therapeutic cranial ionizing radiation is a recognized risk factor for high-grade gliomas generally, and rare cases arise after radiotherapy.
cause_source: WHO CNS5; radiation-induced glioma literature

name: Central neurocytoma
causes: Sporadic tumor of the lateral ventricles with no established cause and no recognized hereditary syndrome or environmental risk factor.
cause_source: WHO CNS5 (central neurocytoma)

name: Hairy cell leukemia
causes: Cause is not established; the near-universal BRAF V600E mutation is a somatic driver, not an external etiology. Some studies report weak associations with prior farming, pesticide, or radiation exposure, but no risk factor is confirmed.
cause_source: WHO Classification of Haematolymphoid Tumours 5th ed; UpToDate

name: Blastic plasmacytoid dendritic cell neoplasm
causes: No established environmental, infectious, or hereditary cause; occurs mainly in older adults and is defined by recurrent deletions (RB1, CDKN2A, TP53). A minority arise after immunosuppression or alongside other myeloid neoplasms.
cause_source: Am J Hematol 2025 BPDCN update; StatPearls (NCBI Bookshelf NBK589661)

name: Acute promyelocytic leukemia
causes: Cause largely not established; defined by the t(15;17) PML::RARA fusion and usually arises de novo, though therapy-related cases occur after prior exposure to topoisomerase II inhibitor chemotherapy.
cause_source: WHO Classification of Haematolymphoid Tumours; NCI PDQ

name: Chronic myelomonocytic leukemia
causes: Most cases are sporadic with no identified cause; established myeloid leukemia risk factors (benzene exposure, cigarette smoking, prior cytotoxic chemotherapy or radiation) contribute to a subset, and it is a clonal disorder of older adults driven by acquired mutations such as TET2, SRSF2, and ASXL1.
cause_source: American Cancer Society AML risk factors; WHO Classification of Haematolymphoid Tumours 5th ed

name: Chronic eosinophilic leukemia
aliases: chronic eosinophilic leukaemia, not otherwise specified
causes: Cause is not established; it is a clonal myeloid proliferation defined by acquired genetic lesions, distinct from the tyrosine kinase fusion driven myeloid neoplasms. No external etiologic factor is proven.
cause_source: WHO Classification of Haematolymphoid Tumours 5th ed

name: T cell prolymphocytic leukemia
causes: Cause largely not established; strongly associated with inversion/translocation of the TCL1 locus, and markedly increased in ataxia-telangiectasia (germline ATM mutation), but sporadic cases have no known external cause.
cause_source: WHO Classification of Haematolymphoid Tumours

name: Waldenstrom macroglobulinemia
causes: Cause not established; nearly all cases carry a somatic MYD88 L265P mutation and it is preceded by IgM monoclonal gammopathy of undetermined significance, with familial clustering suggesting inherited predisposition, but no confirmed environmental cause. Largely sporadic.
cause_source: WHO Classification of Haematolymphoid Tumours; NCI

name: Mantle cell lymphoma
causes: Cause is not established; the defining t(11;14) CCND1 rearrangement is a somatic event. No confirmed infectious, environmental, or hereditary cause, though it is a clonal B-cell malignancy of older adults.
cause_source: WHO Classification of Haematolymphoid Tumours 5th ed; UpToDate

name: Burkitt lymphoma
causes: The endemic (African) form is caused by Epstein-Barr virus with holoendemic Plasmodium falciparum malaria as a cofactor; the immunodeficiency-associated form is linked to HIV infection. The sporadic form has no established cause and is EBV-positive in only a minority.
cause_source: StatPearls Burkitt Lymphoma (NBK538148); WHO Classification 5th ed

name: Anaplastic large cell lymphoma
causes: Cause largely not established for systemic ALK-positive and ALK-negative forms; the breast implant-associated variant is a clear exception, causally linked to textured breast implants with chronic inflammation.
cause_source: WHO Classification of Haematolymphoid Tumours; FDA

name: Mycosis fungoides
causes: Cause is not established; this cutaneous T-cell lymphoma is largely idiopathic. Proposed but unproven contributors include chronic antigen stimulation and environmental or occupational exposures; no infectious agent is confirmed.
cause_source: WHO-EORTC classification of cutaneous lymphomas; UpToDate

name: Sezary syndrome
causes: Cause is not established; the leukemic erythrodermic form of cutaneous T-cell lymphoma is idiopathic with no confirmed infectious or environmental cause.
cause_source: WHO-EORTC classification of cutaneous lymphomas; UpToDate

name: Angioimmunoblastic T cell lymphoma
causes: Cause not established; frequently associated with Epstein-Barr virus in the tumor microenvironment and driven by recurrent T follicular helper cell mutations (TET2, DNMT3A, RHOA, IDH2), but no confirmed etiologic cause. Largely sporadic.
cause_source: WHO Classification of Haematolymphoid Tumours

name: Primary central nervous system lymphoma
causes: Severe immunosuppression is the main established cause, especially HIV/AIDS (with Epstein-Barr virus driving the tumor) and iatrogenic immunosuppression after transplantation; immunocompetent cases are otherwise of unestablished cause.
cause_source: WHO Classification of CNS Tumours; NCI PDQ

name: Hepatosplenic T cell lymphoma
causes: Most cases arise de novo without an identified cause, but roughly 20 percent occur in the setting of chronic immunosuppression or immune dysregulation, notably long-term thiopurine and anti-TNF therapy for inflammatory bowel disease and prior solid organ transplantation.
cause_source: Blood 2020;136:2018 (HSTCL review); Aliment Pharmacol Ther systematic review

name: Extranodal NK/T cell lymphoma
causes: Causally associated with Epstein-Barr virus infection, which is present in essentially all cases; it is most common in East Asian and Latin American populations.
cause_source: WHO Classification of Haematolymphoid Tumours; IARC (EBV)

name: Systemic mastocytosis
causes: Cause not established; the great majority carry an acquired somatic KIT D816V mutation but there is no confirmed inherited or environmental cause, and rare familial cases with germline KIT variants exist. Largely sporadic.
cause_source: WHO Classification of Haematolymphoid Tumours

name: Primary myelofibrosis
causes: Cause is generally not established; it is a clonal myeloproliferative neoplasm driven by acquired JAK2 V617F, CALR, or MPL mutations, which are somatic events. Ionizing radiation and benzene exposure are recognized but uncommon contributors.
cause_source: WHO Classification of Haematolymphoid Tumours 5th ed; UpToDate

name: Polycythemia vera
causes: Cause is not established; it is a clonal myeloproliferative neoplasm caused by an acquired JAK2 mutation (V617F in most cases), which is a somatic driver rather than an external cause. No confirmed environmental etiology.
cause_source: WHO Classification of Haematolymphoid Tumours 5th ed; UpToDate

name: Essential thrombocythemia
causes: Cause is not established; it is a clonal myeloproliferative neoplasm driven by acquired JAK2, CALR, or MPL mutations. These are somatic events, not external causes.
cause_source: WHO Classification of Haematolymphoid Tumours 5th ed; UpToDate

name: Langerhans cell histiocytosis
causes: Now recognized as a clonal neoplasm driven by somatic MAPK pathway mutations (BRAF V600E in about half of cases); these are acquired drivers not external causes. The pulmonary adult form is a distinct entity strongly caused by cigarette (and marijuana) smoking.
cause_source: Blood 2014;124:1119; Front Med 2020 pulmonary LCH review

name: Erdheim-Chester disease
causes: Cause is not an external exposure; it is a clonal histiocytic neoplasm driven by somatic MAPK/ERK pathway mutations, most often BRAF V600E. No infectious, environmental, or hereditary cause is established.
cause_source: Blood 2014;124:1119; WHO Classification of Haematolymphoid Tumours 5th ed

name: Rosai-Dorfman disease
causes: Cause is not established; it is regarded as idiopathic, with a subset harboring somatic MAPK pathway (KRAS, MAP2K1) mutations and rare familial forms tied to SLC29A3 mutations. No confirmed infectious or environmental cause.
cause_source: WHO Classification of Haematolymphoid Tumours 5th ed; Front Med histiocytosis reviews

name: Multicentric Castleman disease
causes: A large fraction is caused by uncontrolled human herpesvirus-8 (HHV-8) infection, typically in the setting of HIV or immunosuppression; the remaining HHV-8-negative idiopathic form has an unknown cause driven by IL-6-mediated cytokine storm.
cause_source: WebSearch (Blood/ASH consensus criteria; Fajgenbaum et al., Blood 2014)

name: Plasmablastic lymphoma
causes: Strongly associated with immunodeficiency, especially HIV/AIDS and iatrogenic immunosuppression after solid organ transplant, and Epstein-Barr virus is detected in most cases; MYC rearrangement is a frequent cofactor. The full pathogenesis remains incompletely defined.
cause_source: Blood 2015;125:2323 (biology of PBL); WHO Classification 5th ed

name: Follicular dendritic cell sarcoma
causes: Cause is generally not established; most are sporadic. A distinct subset, the inflammatory pseudotumor-like variant arising in liver and spleen, is caused by Epstein-Barr virus.
cause_source: WHO Classification of Haematolymphoid Tumours 5th ed; UpToDate

name: Adrenal neuroblastoma
causes: Cause largely not established; most are sporadic embryonal tumors of neural crest origin, with a minority hereditary due to germline ALK mutations or PHOX2B mutations (the latter in congenital central hypoventilation and Hirschsprung syndrome).
cause_source: NCI PDQ Neuroblastoma; WHO Classification

name: Ganglioneuroblastoma
causes: Predominantly sporadic peripheral neuroblastic tumor; a minority relate to the same neuroblastoma predisposition genes (germline ALK or PHOX2B), and no established environmental cause exists.
cause_source: WHO Classification of Tumours; Maris, N Engl J Med 2010

name: Malignant rhabdoid tumor
causes: Germline SMARCB1 (rarely SMARCA4) mutations in rhabdoid tumor predisposition syndrome cause a notable fraction of extracranial cases; the rest are sporadic from biallelic somatic SMARCB1 loss, with no environmental cause established.
cause_source: Eaton et al., Pediatr Blood Cancer 2011; WHO Classification of Tumours

name: Clear cell sarcoma of the kidney
causes: Sporadic pediatric renal tumor with no established environmental, infectious, or hereditary cause; recurrent BCOR internal tandem duplication is a somatic driver, not an etiologic exposure.
cause_source: WHO Classification of Paediatric Tumours; Ueno-Yokohata et al. Nat Genet 2015

name: Germinoma
causes: Most intracranial germinomas are sporadic, but Klinefelter syndrome (sex chromosome aneuploidy with extra X) and Down syndrome are established predisposing conditions that raise the risk of CNS germ cell tumors.
cause_source: Pubmed 18758161; CNS germ cell tumor reviews (Klinefelter/Down associations)

name: Yolk sac tumor
causes: Cause largely not established; this germ cell tumor arises from primordial germ cells and is usually sporadic, with gonadal dysgenesis being a predisposing condition for gonadal cases.
cause_source: WHO Classification of Tumours; NCI PDQ

name: Sacrococcygeal teratoma
causes: Cause not established; this congenital germ cell tumor arises from pluripotent cells near the coccyx (Hensen node remnants) and is generally sporadic, with rare familial presacral teratoma linked to Currarino syndrome (MNX1 mutation).
cause_source: WHO Classification; NCI PDQ Childhood Extracranial Germ Cell Tumors

name: Carcinoma of unknown primary
causes: Cause not established by definition; it is metastatic carcinoma without an identifiable primary site, so no single etiology applies, though it reflects underlying carcinomas whose own risk factors (smoking, alcohol, and others) may be relevant.
cause_source: NCI PDQ Carcinoma of Unknown Primary; ESMO

name: Nevoid basal cell carcinoma syndrome (Gorlin syndrome)
POEMS syndrome
aliases: polyneuropathy organomegaly endocrinopathy monoclonal gammopathy skin changes syndrome

name: Secretory carcinoma
source: WHO Classification of Head and Neck Tumours, 5th ed. (2022)
causes: Cause not established; this salivary and skin tumor is sporadic and defined by an ETV6::NTRK3 fusion, with no known environmental or inherited etiologic factor.
cause_source: WHO 5th ed

name: Salivary duct carcinoma
source: WHO Classification of Head and Neck Tumours, 5th ed. (2022)
causes: Cause not established; largely sporadic, arising de novo or from carcinoma ex pleomorphic adenoma, with prior head and neck radiation a possible minor risk factor.
cause_source: WHO 5th ed

name: Polymorphous adenocarcinoma
source: WHO Classification of Head and Neck Tumours, 5th ed. (2022)
causes: Cause not established; this minor salivary gland carcinoma is sporadic with no known environmental, infectious, or inherited etiology.
cause_source: WHO 5th ed

name: Epithelial-myoepithelial carcinoma
source: WHO Classification of Head and Neck Tumours, 5th ed. (2022)
causes: Cause not established; a rare sporadic salivary gland carcinoma with no proven environmental or inherited cause, though prior head and neck radiation may be a minor risk factor.
cause_source: WHO 5th ed

name: Myoepithelial carcinoma
source: WHO Classification of Head and Neck Tumours, 5th ed. (2022)
causes: Cause not established; sporadic, arising de novo or from a preexisting pleomorphic adenoma or myoepithelioma, with no known environmental or inherited cause.
cause_source: WHO 5th ed

name: Carcinoma ex pleomorphic adenoma
source: WHO Classification of Head and Neck Tumours, 5th ed. (2022)
causes: Arises from malignant transformation of a preexisting benign pleomorphic adenoma, so a long-standing or recurrent pleomorphic adenoma is the established precursor lesion; prior radiation is a minor risk factor.
cause_source: WHO 5th ed

name: Basal cell adenocarcinoma
source: WHO Classification of Head and Neck Tumours, 5th ed. (2022)
causes: Cause not established; usually sporadic, though rare cases arise in patients with membranous basal cell adenoma and hereditary cylindromatosis (CYLD-related), otherwise no known cause.
cause_source: WHO 5th ed

name: Clear cell carcinoma
source: WHO Classification of Head and Neck Tumours, 5th ed. (2022)
causes: Cause not established; this salivary gland carcinoma (EWSR1::ATF1 fusion) is sporadic with no known environmental or inherited etiologic factor.
cause_source: WHO 5th ed

name: Intraductal carcinoma
source: WHO Classification of Head and Neck Tumours, 5th ed. (2022)
causes: Cause not established; a rare sporadic salivary gland carcinoma with no known environmental, infectious, or inherited cause.
cause_source: WHO 5th ed

name: Sclerosing microcystic adenocarcinoma
source: WHO Classification of Head and Neck Tumours, 5th ed. (2022)
causes: Cause not established; a very rare sporadic salivary gland carcinoma with no known environmental or inherited etiology.
cause_source: WHO 5th ed

name: Microsecretory adenocarcinoma
source: WHO Classification of Head and Neck Tumours, 5th ed. (2022)
causes: Cause not established; a recently described rare sporadic salivary gland carcinoma (MEF2C::SS18 fusion) with no known environmental or inherited cause.
cause_source: WHO 5th ed

name: Mucinous adenocarcinoma
source: WHO Classification of Head and Neck Tumours, 5th ed. (2022)
causes: Cause not established; salivary mucinous adenocarcinoma is a rare sporadic tumor (recurrent AKT1 mutations described) with no known environmental or inherited etiologic factor.
cause_source: WHO 5th ed

name: Sialoblastoma
source: WHO Classification of Head and Neck Tumours, 5th ed. (2022)
causes: Cause not established; a rare congenital or perinatal salivary gland tumor of presumed embryonal origin, sporadic, with no proven inherited or environmental cause.
cause_source: WHO 5th ed / Orphanet

name: Lymphoepithelial carcinoma
source: WHO Classification of Head and Neck Tumours, 5th ed. (2022)
causes: Strongly associated with Epstein-Barr virus, particularly in endemic populations (for example southern Chinese and Inuit), where the virus is present in a clonal form within the tumor cells and is implicated in oncogenesis.
cause_source: PMID 8543624 / WHO 5th ed

name: Squamous cell carcinoma of salivary gland
source: WHO Classification of Head and Neck Tumours, 5th ed. (2022)
causes: Primary salivary squamous cell carcinoma is rare and largely sporadic; prior head and neck radiation is a recognized risk factor, and metastasis or extension of cutaneous squamous cell carcinoma must be excluded.
cause_source: WHO 5th ed

name: Salivary carcinosarcoma
source: WHO Classification of Head and Neck Tumours, 5th ed. (2022)
causes: Cause not established; usually arises from a preexisting pleomorphic adenoma (true malignant mixed tumor) but is otherwise sporadic with no known environmental or inherited cause.
cause_source: WHO 5th ed

name: SMARCB1-deficient sinonasal carcinoma
source: WHO Classification of Head and Neck Tumours, 5th ed. (2022)
causes: Cause not established; a sporadic sinonasal carcinoma defined by somatic biallelic SMARCB1 (INI1) inactivation, with no known environmental, infectious, or inherited etiologic factor.
cause_source: WHO 5th ed

name: SMARCA4-deficient sinonasal carcinoma
source: WHO Classification of Head and Neck Tumours, 5th ed. (2022)
causes: Cause not established; a sporadic sinonasal carcinoma defined by somatic SMARCA4 (BRG1) loss, with no known environmental or inherited etiologic factor.
cause_source: WHO 5th ed

name: HPV-related multiphenotypic sinonasal carcinoma
aliases: HPV-related carcinoma with adenoid cystic-like features
source: WHO Classification of Head and Neck Tumours, 5th ed. (2022)
causes: Caused by high-risk human papillomavirus infection (most often HPV type 33, also 16, 31, 35, 52, 56), which is consistently present and drives this sinonasal carcinoma.
cause_source: PMID 30259271 / WHO 5th ed

name: Sinonasal teratocarcinosarcoma
source: WHO Classification of Head and Neck Tumours, 5th ed. (2022)
causes: Cause not established; a rare aggressive sinonasal tumor of mixed epithelial, mesenchymal, and neuroepithelial elements that is sporadic with no known environmental or inherited cause.
cause_source: WHO 5th ed

name: Intestinal-type sinonasal adenocarcinoma
source: WHO Classification of Head and Neck Tumours, 5th ed. (2022)
causes: Occupational exposure to hardwood dust and leather (tanning) dust is the established cause, with these tumors classically arising in woodworkers and leather workers after long latency.
cause_source: IARC Monographs / WHO 5th ed

name: Non-intestinal-type sinonasal adenocarcinoma
source: WHO Classification of Head and Neck Tumours, 5th ed. (2022)
causes: Cause largely not established; most cases are sporadic, though some sinonasal adenocarcinomas are linked to wood dust and other occupational inhalant exposures.
cause_source: WHO 5th ed

name: Sinonasal neuroendocrine carcinoma
source: WHO Classification of Head and Neck Tumours, 5th ed. (2022)
causes: Cause not established; a rare sporadic sinonasal carcinoma with no proven environmental, infectious, or inherited etiologic factor.
cause_source: WHO 5th ed

name: Respiratory epithelial adenomatoid hamartoma-associated adenocarcinoma
source: WHO Classification of Head and Neck Tumours, 5th ed. (2022)
causes: Arises in association with a preexisting respiratory epithelial adenomatoid hamartoma, which serves as a precursor lesion; the underlying cause of the hamartoma itself is not established.
cause_source: WHO 5th ed

name: Middle ear adenocarcinoma
aliases: aggressive papillary tumor of middle ear (obsolete)
source: WHO Classification of Head and Neck Tumours, 5th ed. (2022)
causes: Cause not established; a very rare sporadic tumor of the middle ear with no known environmental, infectious, or inherited cause.
cause_source: WHO 5th ed

name: Endolymphatic sac tumor
source: WHO Classification of Head and Neck Tumours, 5th ed. (2022)
causes: Frequently caused by von Hippel-Lindau disease, an inherited VHL tumor suppressor syndrome; up to about 16 percent of VHL patients develop these tumors through biallelic VHL inactivation, while other cases are sporadic.
cause_source: NEJM 2004 (PMID 15035284) / WHO 5th ed

name: Middle ear squamous cell carcinoma
aliases: external auditory canal squamous cell carcinoma
source: WHO Classification of Head and Neck Tumours, 5th ed. (2022)
causes: Chronic long-standing suppurative otitis media and inflammation are the main recognized predisposing conditions; prior radiation to the region is an additional risk factor.
cause_source: WHO 5th ed / NCI

name: Ceruminous adenocarcinoma
aliases: ceruminous gland carcinoma of external auditory canal
source: WHO Classification of Head and Neck Tumours, 5th ed. (2022)
causes: Cause not established; a rare sporadic adenocarcinoma of the ceruminous glands of the external auditory canal with no known environmental or inherited cause.
cause_source: WHO 5th ed

name: Laryngeal neuroendocrine carcinoma
source: WHO Classification of Head and Neck Tumours, 5th ed. (2022)
causes: Tobacco smoking is a recognized risk factor for laryngeal neuroendocrine carcinomas, consistent with other smoking-related aerodigestive neuroendocrine tumors; otherwise many cases have no established cause.
cause_source: WHO 5th ed

name: Spindle cell squamous cell carcinoma of the larynx
source: WHO Classification of Head and Neck Tumours, 5th ed. (2022)
causes: Tobacco smoking and alcohol use are the established risk factors, shared with conventional laryngeal squamous cell carcinoma; prior radiation is an additional risk factor.
cause_source: WHO 5th ed / NCI

name: Thymoma type AB
source: WHO Classification of Thoracic Tumours, 5th ed. (2021)
causes: Cause not established; like other thymomas it is sporadic with no known environmental or inherited cause, though it may associate with paraneoplastic autoimmune disease.
cause_source: WHO 5th ed

name: Thymoma type B1
source: WHO Classification of Thoracic Tumours, 5th ed. (2021)
causes: Cause not established; sporadic with no known environmental or inherited cause, commonly associated with myasthenia gravis as a paraneoplastic association rather than a cause.
cause_source: WHO 5th ed

name: Thymoma type B2
source: WHO Classification of Thoracic Tumours, 5th ed. (2021)
causes: Cause not established; sporadic with no known environmental or inherited cause, frequently associated with myasthenia gravis as an association not a cause.
cause_source: WHO 5th ed

name: Thymoma type B3
source: WHO Classification of Thoracic Tumours, 5th ed. (2021)
causes: Cause not established; sporadic with no known environmental or inherited cause, often associated with myasthenia gravis as a paraneoplastic association.
cause_source: WHO 5th ed

name: Micronodular thymoma with lymphoid stroma
source: WHO Classification of Thoracic Tumours, 5th ed. (2021)
causes: Cause not established; a rare sporadic thymoma variant with no known environmental or inherited cause.
cause_source: WHO 5th ed

name: Metaplastic thymoma
source: WHO Classification of Thoracic Tumours, 5th ed. (2021)
causes: Cause not established; a rare sporadic biphasic thymoma (recurrent YAP1::MAML2 fusion) with no known environmental or inherited cause.
cause_source: WHO 5th ed

name: Thymic neuroendocrine tumor
source: WHO Classification of Thoracic Tumours, 5th ed. (2021)
causes: Mostly sporadic; a substantial minority arise in the setting of inherited multiple endocrine neoplasia type 1 (MEN1), which is an established predisposing syndrome, and there is an association with tobacco smoking.
cause_source: WHO 5th ed / NCI

name: Pericardial mesothelioma
source: WHO Classification of Thoracic Tumours, 5th ed. (2021)
causes: Largely sporadic and its link to asbestos is weaker than for pleural mesothelioma; asbestos exposure and prior radiation are possible risk factors but a cause is often not established in individual cases.
cause_source: WHO 5th ed / NCI

name: Mesothelioma of the tunica vaginalis
source: WHO Classification of Urinary and Male Genital Tumours, 5th ed. (2022)
causes: Asbestos exposure is implicated in a subset, and prior scrotal trauma, hydrocele, or hernia have been proposed as predisposing conditions, but many cases have no established cause.
cause_source: WHO 5th ed / NCI

name: Well-differentiated papillary mesothelial tumor
source: WHO Classification of Thoracic Tumours, 5th ed. (2021)
causes: Cause not established; most are sporadic with an uncertain and generally weak relationship to asbestos, and no proven inherited cause.
cause_source: WHO 5th ed

name: Pulmonary blastoma
source: WHO Classification of Thoracic Tumours, 5th ed. (2021)
causes: Cause not established; a rare biphasic lung tumor that is largely sporadic, though tobacco smoking has been reported in some patients, with no proven inherited cause.
cause_source: WHO 5th ed

name: Pulmonary sarcomatoid carcinoma
source: WHO Classification of Thoracic Tumours, 5th ed. (2021)
causes: Tobacco smoking is the established main risk factor, as for other non-small cell lung carcinomas; asbestos exposure has also been reported.
cause_source: WHO 5th ed / NCI

name: Pulmonary carcinosarcoma
source: WHO Classification of Thoracic Tumours, 5th ed. (2021)
causes: Tobacco smoking is the established main risk factor, consistent with its biphasic non-small cell lung carcinoma nature.
cause_source: WHO 5th ed / NCI

name: Pulmonary mucoepidermoid carcinoma
aliases: salivary gland-type mucoepidermoid carcinoma of lung
source: WHO Classification of Thoracic Tumours, 5th ed. (2021)
causes: Cause not established; this bronchial salivary-type carcinoma is sporadic and not clearly linked to smoking, with no known inherited or environmental cause.
cause_source: WHO 5th ed

name: Pulmonary adenoid cystic carcinoma
aliases: salivary gland-type adenoid cystic carcinoma of lung
source: WHO Classification of Thoracic Tumours, 5th ed. (2021)
causes: Cause not established; this tracheobronchial salivary-type carcinoma is sporadic and not clearly related to smoking, with no known environmental or inherited cause.
cause_source: WHO 5th ed

name: Pulmonary epithelial-myoepithelial carcinoma
aliases: salivary gland-type epithelial-myoepithelial carcinoma of lung
source: WHO Classification of Thoracic Tumours, 5th ed. (2021)
causes: Cause not established; a very rare sporadic bronchial salivary-type carcinoma with no known environmental or inherited cause.
cause_source: WHO 5th ed

name: Pulmonary hyalinizing clear cell carcinoma
aliases: salivary gland-type hyalinizing clear cell carcinoma of lung
source: WHO Classification of Thoracic Tumours, 5th ed. (2021)
causes: Cause not established; a rare sporadic salivary-type lung carcinoma (EWSR1::ATF1 fusion) with no known environmental or inherited cause.
cause_source: WHO 5th ed

name: Pulmonary NUT carcinoma
source: WHO Classification of Thoracic Tumours, 5th ed. (2021)
causes: Cause not established; arises from a sporadic somatic NUTM1 rearrangement with no known environmental, lifestyle, or inherited risk factor and no consistent link to smoking.
cause_source: PMC8966081 / WHO 5th ed

name: Pulmonary lymphoepithelial carcinoma
source: WHO Classification of Thoracic Tumours, 5th ed. (2021)
causes: Strongly associated with Epstein-Barr virus in Asian patients, where over 90 percent of cases carry EBV within the tumor cells; in Western populations the EBV association is much weaker.
cause_source: PMC12380799 / WHO 5th ed

name: Pulmonary typical carcinoid
source: WHO Classification of Thoracic Tumours, 5th ed. (2021)
causes: Largely sporadic and not clearly related to smoking; a minority arise in inherited multiple endocrine neoplasia type 1 (MEN1), an established predisposing syndrome, or in diffuse idiopathic pulmonary neuroendocrine cell hyperplasia.
cause_source: WHO 5th ed / NCI

name: Pulmonary atypical carcinoid
source: WHO Classification of Thoracic Tumours, 5th ed. (2021)
causes: Largely sporadic; a minority are associated with inherited MEN1 syndrome, and unlike typical carcinoid there is some association with tobacco smoking.
cause_source: WHO 5th ed / NCI

name: Large cell neuroendocrine carcinoma of the lung
source: WHO Classification of Thoracic Tumours, 5th ed. (2021)
causes: Tobacco smoking is the established main cause, as for other high-grade pulmonary neuroendocrine carcinomas.
cause_source: WHO 5th ed / NCI

name: Fetal adenocarcinoma of the lung
aliases: pulmonary endodermal tumor resembling fetal lung
source: WHO Classification of Thoracic Tumours, 5th ed. (2021)
causes: Cause not established; a rare lung adenocarcinoma variant that is largely sporadic (well-differentiated form has recurrent CTNNB1 mutations), though smoking has been reported in the high-grade form.
cause_source: WHO 5th ed

name: Tracheal squamous cell carcinoma
source: WHO Classification of Thoracic Tumours, 5th ed. (2021)
causes: Tobacco smoking is the established main risk factor, shared with other aerodigestive squamous cell carcinomas.
cause_source: WHO 5th ed / NCI

name: Tracheal adenoid cystic carcinoma
source: WHO Classification of Thoracic Tumours, 5th ed. (2021)
causes: Cause not established; this tracheal salivary-type carcinoma is sporadic and not related to smoking, with no known environmental or inherited cause.
cause_source: WHO 5th ed

name: Esophageal small cell neuroendocrine carcinoma
aliases: Small cell carcinoma of the esophagus
source: WHO Classification of Tumours 5th ed, Digestive System
causes: Rare and aggressive; shares risk factors with esophageal squamous cell carcinoma including tobacco smoking and heavy alcohol use, though the specific etiology of the neuroendocrine phenotype is not well established.
cause_source: WHO 5th ed; PMID 28881922

name: Esophageal adenoid cystic carcinoma
aliases: Adenoid cystic carcinoma of the esophagus
source: WHO Classification of Tumours 5th ed, Digestive System
causes: Extremely rare tumor arising from esophageal submucosal glands; no established risk factor, carcinogen, or predisposing condition has been identified and the cause is genuinely unknown.
cause_source: WHO 5th ed; DoveMed rare disease reference

name: Esophageal undifferentiated carcinoma
source: WHO Classification of Tumours 5th ed, Digestive System
causes: A rare high-grade esophageal carcinoma; it is presumed to share the major esophageal carcinogenic exposures (tobacco, alcohol for squamous-type; reflux/Barrett esophagus for glandular-type), but a specific established cause for the undifferentiated form is not defined.
cause_source: WHO 5th ed

name: Hepatoid adenocarcinoma of the stomach
source: WHO Classification of Tumours 5th ed, Digestive System
causes: A rare alpha-fetoprotein producing gastric adenocarcinoma variant; it shares the general risk profile of gastric cancer (Helicobacter pylori infection, chronic atrophic gastritis) but no cause specific to the hepatoid phenotype is established.
cause_source: WHO 5th ed; PMID 24669215

name: Type 1 gastric neuroendocrine tumor
source: WHO Classification of Tumours 5th ed, Digestive System
causes: Caused by autoimmune chronic atrophic gastritis: destruction of oxyntic mucosa raises gastrin, and chronic hypergastrinemia drives enterochromaffin-like cell hyperplasia and neoplasia; strongly linked to pernicious anemia and autoimmune metaplastic atrophic gastritis.
cause_source: WHO 5th ed; NCI

name: Type 2 gastric neuroendocrine tumor
source: WHO Classification of Tumours 5th ed, Digestive System
causes: Caused by hypergastrinemia from a gastrinoma in the setting of Zollinger-Ellison syndrome, almost always within multiple endocrine neoplasia type 1 (MEN1); excess gastrin drives enterochromaffin-like cell proliferation.
cause_source: WHO 5th ed; NCI

name: Type 3 gastric neuroendocrine tumor
source: WHO Classification of Tumours 5th ed, Digestive System
causes: Sporadic gastrin-independent tumor arising on a background of normal gastric mucosa and normal serum gastrin; no chronic gastritis or hypergastrinemia driver and no established external cause, so the etiology is not established.
cause_source: WHO 5th ed; PMID 30528432

name: Small intestinal adenocarcinoma
source: WHO Classification of Tumours 5th ed, Digestive System; RARECAREnet
causes: Predisposing conditions are well established: Crohn disease and celiac disease (chronic small bowel inflammation), and hereditary syndromes familial adenomatous polyposis, Lynch syndrome, and Peutz-Jeghers syndrome; most tumors arise from precursor adenomas.
cause_source: WHO 5th ed; NCI

name: Low-grade appendiceal mucinous neoplasm
source: WHO Classification of Tumours 5th ed, Digestive System
causes: Sporadic mucinous epithelial neoplasm of the appendix commonly harboring KRAS/GNAS mutations; no established environmental cause, infectious agent, or hereditary predisposition, so the etiology is not established.
cause_source: WHO 5th ed; PMID 26260910

name: High-grade appendiceal mucinous neoplasm
source: WHO Classification of Tumours 5th ed, Digestive System
causes: Sporadic high-grade mucinous neoplasm of the appendix; like its low-grade counterpart it has no established external cause or hereditary predisposition and the etiology is not established.
cause_source: WHO 5th ed

name: Appendiceal goblet cell adenocarcinoma
source: WHO Classification of Tumours 5th ed, Digestive System
causes: Rare appendiceal tumor with mixed goblet cell and adenocarcinomatous features; it is essentially sporadic with no established causal exposure or hereditary syndrome, so the etiology is not established.
cause_source: WHO 5th ed; NCBI StatPearls Appendiceal Tumors

name: Appendiceal neuroendocrine tumor
source: WHO Classification of Tumours 5th ed, Digestive System
causes: Usually an incidental sporadic tumor of the appendiceal tip; no established environmental cause and no consistent hereditary predisposition, so the etiology is not established.
cause_source: WHO 5th ed; NCI

name: Appendiceal mucinous adenocarcinoma
source: WHO Classification of Tumours 5th ed, Digestive System
causes: Sporadic invasive mucinous adenocarcinoma of the appendix, often KRAS/GNAS mutated; no established carcinogen, infection, or hereditary cause, so the etiology is not established.
cause_source: WHO 5th ed

name: Anal melanoma
source: WHO Classification of Tumours 5th ed, Digestive System; NCI
causes: Rare mucosal melanoma of the anorectum; unlike cutaneous melanoma it is not linked to ultraviolet exposure, and no established environmental or hereditary cause has been identified, so the etiology is not established.
cause_source: WHO 5th ed; NCI

name: Perianal Paget disease
aliases: Extramammary Paget disease of the anus
source: WHO Classification of Tumours 5th ed, Digestive System
causes: Intraepithelial adenocarcinoma of perianal skin; a substantial proportion is secondary to an underlying anorectal or other visceral adenocarcinoma spreading to the epidermis, while primary cases arising from apocrine gland cells have no established external cause.
cause_source: WHO 5th ed; PMID 22832497

name: Perihilar cholangiocarcinoma
source: WHO Classification of Tumours 5th ed, Digestive System
causes: Established risk factors include primary sclerosing cholangitis, chronic biliary inflammation, choledochal cysts and Caroli disease, hepatolithiasis, and in endemic regions liver fluke infection (Opisthorchis viverrini, Clonorchis sinensis); chronic biliary tract inflammation is the unifying mechanism.
cause_source: WHO 5th ed; NCI

name: Distal cholangiocarcinoma
source: WHO Classification of Tumours 5th ed, Digestive System
causes: Shares biliary risk factors with other cholangiocarcinomas: primary sclerosing cholangitis, choledochal cysts, hepatolithiasis, chronic biliary inflammation, and liver fluke infection (Opisthorchis viverrini, Clonorchis sinensis) in endemic areas.
cause_source: WHO 5th ed; NCI

name: Combined hepatocellular-cholangiocarcinoma
source: WHO Classification of Tumours 5th ed, Digestive System
causes: A biphenotypic primary liver cancer that arises on the same backgrounds as hepatocellular carcinoma, notably cirrhosis from chronic hepatitis B or C infection, alcohol-related liver disease, and other chronic liver injury.
cause_source: WHO 5th ed; PMID 30886459

name: Gallbladder carcinoma
source: WHO Classification of Tumours 5th ed, Digestive System; RARECAREnet
causes: Strongly caused by chronic gallbladder inflammation, most often from gallstones (cholelithiasis) and chronic cholecystitis; other risk factors include porcelain gallbladder, gallbladder polyps, chronic Salmonella typhi carriage, and anomalous pancreaticobiliary junction.
cause_source: WHO 5th ed; NCI

name: Solid pseudopapillary neoplasm of the pancreas
source: WHO Classification of Tumours 5th ed, Digestive System
causes: Low-grade pancreatic neoplasm overwhelmingly in young women, defined by somatic CTNNB1 (beta-catenin) mutations; despite hypotheses about female sex hormones, there is no established etiologic cause and it is considered idiopathic/sporadic.
cause_source: WHO 5th ed; PMID 35047123

name: Insulinoma
source: WHO Classification of Tumours 5th ed, Digestive System; Orphanet
causes: Usually a sporadic pancreatic neuroendocrine tumor with no established external cause, but about 5 to 10 percent occur in multiple endocrine neoplasia type 1 (MEN1); the sporadic cases are considered idiopathic.
cause_source: WHO 5th ed; NCI

name: Gastrinoma
source: WHO Classification of Tumours 5th ed, Digestive System; Orphanet
causes: Gastrin-secreting neuroendocrine tumor of the pancreas or duodenum; roughly a quarter arise in multiple endocrine neoplasia type 1 (MEN1), which is an established hereditary cause, while the remaining sporadic tumors have no established external cause.
cause_source: WHO 5th ed; NCI

name: VIPoma
source: WHO Classification of Tumours 5th ed, Digestive System; Orphanet
causes: Rare vasoactive intestinal peptide secreting pancreatic neuroendocrine tumor; mostly sporadic with no established external cause, though a minority occur in multiple endocrine neoplasia type 1 (MEN1).
cause_source: WHO 5th ed; PMID 28143751

name: Glucagonoma
source: WHO Classification of Tumours 5th ed, Digestive System; Orphanet
causes: Rare glucagon-secreting pancreatic neuroendocrine tumor; usually sporadic with no established external cause, occasionally associated with multiple endocrine neoplasia type 1 (MEN1).
cause_source: WHO 5th ed; NCI

name: Somatostatinoma
source: WHO Classification of Tumours 5th ed, Digestive System; Orphanet
causes: Rare somatostatin-secreting neuroendocrine tumor of the pancreas or duodenum; associations exist with neurofibromatosis type 1 (especially duodenal tumors) and multiple endocrine neoplasia type 1, but most pancreatic cases are sporadic with no established external cause.
cause_source: WHO 5th ed; PMID 28143751

name: Serous cystadenocarcinoma of the pancreas
source: WHO Classification of Tumours 5th ed, Digestive System
causes: Exceedingly rare malignant counterpart of serous cystadenoma; benign serous cystic neoplasms are linked to von Hippel-Lindau disease, but for the very rare carcinoma there is no established cause, so the etiology is not established.
cause_source: WHO 5th ed; PMID 22475742

name: Gastroenteropancreatic neuroendocrine carcinoma
source: WHO Classification of Tumours 5th ed, Digestive System
causes: High-grade neuroendocrine carcinoma of the GI tract or pancreas; predominantly sporadic with no established external cause, and unlike well-differentiated tumors it is rarely syndrome-associated, so the etiology is not established.
cause_source: WHO 5th ed; NCI

name: Mixed neuroendocrine-non-neuroendocrine neoplasm
source: WHO Classification of Tumours 5th ed, Digestive System
causes: A composite tumor combining neuroendocrine and non-neuroendocrine (usually adenocarcinoma) components; it is sporadic and thought to derive from a common precursor, with no established distinct etiologic cause.
cause_source: WHO 5th ed; PMID 27561810

name: PDGFRA-mutant gastrointestinal stromal tumor
source: WHO Classification of Tumours 5th ed, Digestive System
causes: A molecular subtype of GIST driven by a somatic activating PDGFRA mutation (commonly D842V); it is sporadic with no established external or hereditary cause, and the mutation is a driver rather than an etiologic exposure.
cause_source: WHO 5th ed; NCI

name: SDH-deficient gastrointestinal stromal tumor
source: WHO Classification of Tumours 5th ed, Digestive System; Boikos JAMA Oncol 2016 (PMID 27011036)
